draft.introduction: The Vendidad (Videvdat) is the Zoroastrian law code against demons, containing purification rituals, creation myths, and the geography of the sixteen lands created by Ahura Mazda. It is the most extensive surviving text of the Avestan legal tradition.

translation.text: Ahura Mazda spoke to Spitama Zarathustra, saying: ‘I have made every land dear to its dwellers, even though it had no charms. If I had not made every land dear to its dwellers, the whole living world would have invaded the Airyana Vaejah.’ The first of the good lands I created was Airyana Vaejah by the river Vanguhi Daitya.

translator_notes.note: The sixteen lands created by Ahura Mazda and subsequently afflicted by Angra Mainyu establish the Zoroastrian cosmic dualism: every good creation is countered by evil. The Airyana Vaejah (Iranian homeland) as a paradise corrupted by harsh winters reflects both mythic geography and the Indo-Iranian migration experience.
